pubmed_links: [Wilson & Litwa (2021) Synaptic hyaluronan synthesis and CD44-mediated signaling coordinate neural circuit development](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/34685554/); [Kruk et al. (2023) Astrocytic CD44 deficiency reduces the severity of kainate-induced epilepsy](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37296604/); [Bausch (2006) Potential roles for hyaluronan and CD44 in kainic acid-induced mossy fiber sprouting](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/16949761/)
summary: CD44, acting as the primary receptor for hyaluronan in the brain, suppresses excitatory synaptogenesis during neural circuit development via RhoGTPase/Rac1 signaling, and disruption of this HA-CD44 axis is sufficient to produce neural hyperexcitability associated with epilepsy, intellectual disability, and autism spectrum disorder; astrocytic CD44 additionally amplifies reactive astrogliosis following seizures, worsening epileptic outcomes. CD44 has not been identified as a direct genetic risk factor in GWAS or sequencing studies of neuropsychiatric disorders. (novel candidate)
